untranslatable_reason: This signal is fundamentally order-book microstructure logic (time precedence, price improvement by tick increments, bid-ask spread mechanics) that cannot be expressed using only price/technical indicators. The rule requires: (1) explicit order book state tracking (existing bids/offers with timestamps), (2) tick-level price improvement calculations relative to current bid-ask spread, (3) order execution priority mechanics. These require real-time order book data and execution logic unavailable in a candle-based backtest engine.
translation_notes: Signal MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_6_1 describes an execution tactic for beating time-precedence orders through price improvement—this is an order placement strategy, not a market timing signal. A backtest engine operating on OHLCV bars cannot simulate order book priority, tick-level quoting, or execution priority rules. This belongs in an order management system (OMS) or limit order book simulator, not a technical pattern backtest.
